item.title: NYT Connections Hints Today (#1055)—Answers for Friday, May 1
item.content:encoded:
It’s a whole new month! Do you agree that 2026 is unravelling far too quickly? We’re already welcoming May, and it feels like just weeks ago that we were ushering in the new year. Let’s start this month the right way, by keeping up that NYT Connections streak. In this guide, you’ll pick up the best NYT Connections hints and solutions you can find anywhere online.

How to Play NYT Connections

In June 2023, NYT Connections emerged as yet another game in the ever-growing lineup of the New York Times titles. This title soared immediately, and it’s easy to see why. As a free-to-play, accessible New York Times game, NYT Connections is available worldwide and can be played on desktop devices or in a mobile app.

But how do you play NYT Connections?

The NYT team will provide you with a grid of 16 words once a day. The only task left up to you is to sort those words into four groups of four by finding their commonalities. Every NYT Connections puzzle has just one solution, and some words can have double meanings, so it can sometimes be tricky to figure out the answers.

It’s also important to stress that the words are color-coded: yellow, blue, green, and purple. That’s in order of difficulty, from least to most.

What’s critical here is that you get four guesses every day, and if you use those, your NYT Connections answers for today will be out of reach, as it’ll be game over.

Today’s NYT Connections Hints (May 1)

Here are the words from today’s NYT Connections puzzle:

  • POUR
  • WAX
  • ALE
  • HIVE
  • CREST
  • HONEY
  • MIX
  • COMB
  • BUFF
  • WIGHT
  • WATTLE
  • CITRINE
  • SHINE
  • BEAK
  • AMBER
  • POLISH

And here are the hints I can offer for today’s NYT Connections:

  • Yellow – Things you’d do to make something glossy
  • Green – Different terms for hues of gold
  • Blue – Things you’d find on a bird’s head
  • Purple – Numbers with a single letter changed

Here’s the final warning before we get to the NYT Connections answers for today, May 1:

Are you sure you want to scroll further?

Very well!

NYT Connections Answers Explained for Today, May 1 (#1055)

If you want to jump straight to the answers for today’s NYT Connections puzzle, I’ve got you covered:

  • Yellow – BUFF, POLISH, SHINE, WAX
  • Green – ALE, AMBER, CITRINE, HONEY
  • Blue – BEAK, COMB, CREST, WATTLE
  • Purple – HIVE, MIX, POUR, WIGHT

When Does NYT Connections Reset?

That’s today’s puzzle, but if you want the next one, the game resets daily at midnight local time (that’s your local time). You’ll have 24 hours to complete the puzzle when midnight ticks over in your timezone.

How did you do? Did you get a perfect run? Let me know what your streak looks like on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

MOBILE APP

item.title: Michael B. Jordan’s Battlefield Movie Ignites Reported Bidding War
item.content:encoded:
A Battlefield bidding war has ensued as the video game adaptation has five top-tier studios vying for the rights to the film.

Electronic Arts‘ lucrative first-person shooter video game franchise is being adapted into a new film, with Michael B. Jordan attached. Jordan, who recently won an Oscar for his role in Sinners, is slated to produce and potentially star in the film. Now, a new report shows just how much interest there is in acquiring the film’s rights.

Battlefield Movie Sparks ‘Bidding War of the Year’

According to TheWrap, Warner Bros. Discovery, Amazon MGM Studios, Sony, Universal, and Netflix have all submitted bids for the rights to adapt the military shooter into a video game. The report even dubs the negotiations as “shaping up to be the biggest bidding war of the year.”

In addition to the aforementioned Michael B. Jordan, Christopher McQuarrie is also attached to the project. McQuarrie is slated to write, direct, and produce the project. He’s known for writing films such as Top Gun: Maverick and several Mission: Impossible movies. He’s also listed as a writer on the 1995 film The Usual Suspects.

Battlefield Is Far From the Only Forthcoming Video Game Adaptation

In 2026, it seems video game properties are primarily focused on expanding into other media. God of War, Atomfall, Assassin’s Creed, and Fallout are all major video game franchises that have received TV adaptations. Learn more about the recently announced Atomfall adaptation here.

Meanwhile, The Legend of Zelda, Super Mario, Sonic the Hedgehog, Resident Evil, and more are all set to appear in theaters. The teaser trailer for the upcoming Resident Evil film just dropped and can be seen here.

Insider Gaming will continue to monitor everything regarding the upcoming Battlefield film and report any updates.

What are you hoping to see from a Battlefield movie? Sound off about this story and all others in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

item.title: Subnautica 2 Enters Early Access in May
item.content:encoded:
Subnautica 2 will be entering Early Access on all PC gaming platforms, including Steam, the Epic Game Store, and Xbox Game Pass, on May 14. The announcement was made by the game’s developer, Unknown Worlds, on April 30.

The game is crafted as a solo experience, as was the 2018 predecessor. However, the option is there to play with up to three friends in online co-op. The developers tout the release of multiple characters and customization options throughout the Early Access experience.

“Driven from your home by ongoing conflict, Alterra offers you the chance at a new life. But as the colony ship CICADA shepherds you and your fellow Pioneers to your new home, something goes awry. The ship’s AI insists that your mission should continue. Stranded and faced with near-insurmountable odds, you must do everything in your power to survive. The future of humanity on this world is in your hands,” reads the game’s synopsis on Steam.

Outbound Moving Up PC Release to Avoid Subnautica 2

The much-anticipated release from Unknown Worlds has prompted at least one independent developer to release sooner to avoid being swept up in the hype for Subnautica 2. Outbound, a colorful and cozy base-building game centered around a camper van, will now be released for PC platforms on May 11 instead of May 14. The game will still be coming to home consoles on the 14.

Square Glade Games made the announcement shortly after it was revealed that Subnautica 2 would be entering Early Access on the 14. In a Steam message, the development team joked that they had to avoid the Leviathan.

“The highly anticipated title Subnautica 2 just revealed their release date – which falls exactly on our planned release date. We need to dodge the Leviathan, which means that you can start your road trip on earlier! With Subnautica 2 launching on May 14th on Steam and Xbox, Outbound will now release earlier on May 11th on Steam, Epic Games Store, and Xbox – with the other consoles following on May 14th!”

Are you excited for the upcoming release of Subnautica 2? Does Outbound look like a game more your speed? Sound off about this story and all others in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

item.title: NYT Strands #788 Hints and Answers for Today, April 30, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Strands is a daily word search puzzle game from The New York Times. It is a relatively new game, launched in 2024, in which players seek a common theme among words in a 6×8 letter grid.

Whether it’s your first time playing or you’re a Strands veteran, today’s tips and strategies can help you find words faster. So, if you’ve found yourself stuck with today’s Strands, don’t worry, below you’ll find some helpful hints and even the answers for Strands Game #788 for Thursday, April 30, 2026

To play today’s Strands, head to the game’s website.

IMPORTANT: The following information includes spoilers on today’s edition of Strands. If you don’t want the answer given to you, stop scrolling.

How To Play Strands

Strands is a daily word search that challenges you to find words with a common theme. There’s also a Spanagram that reveals the deeper theme behind the words.

  • Words that are three letters and under do not count.
  • Words that reveal the correct theme appear in blue.
  • The Spanagram turns yellow when found.
  • Finding three words that don’t fit the theme will reveal a hint to help you find the proper words.

The goal is to string together as many words as possible. Every letter on the grid gets used! Daily puzzles keep players coming back for more, making it a fun brain exercise and a popular online trend.

Strands can only be played on the New York Times website or on the NYT Games app.

Strands Hints For Today, April 30, 2026

The Strands’ answers to the theme of Game #788 are all related to moisture.

General Strands Hints For April 30

Without giving today’s Strands answers completely away, here are a few helpful hints for today to get you started:

  • Hint 1: Valve’s PC gaming platform.
  • Hint 2: A type of music that sounds like it was born from ’80s video game option menus, and the ’90s and Y2K aesthetic associated with it, is called _ wave.
  • Hint 3: The spanagram is what you call the fog on your window on a rainy day. Family Guy once did a cutaway gag about it.

Use these clues to narrow down your searches and increase your chances of solving Strands #788 for April 30, 2026.

Words to Help You Find Strand Hints for April 30, 2026

If you want help getting started without having today’s Strands answer given to you, there are a few starter words worth trying that will help you unlock some in-game hints. A few starter words that may help are:

  • PITS
  • SPIT
  • PAIN
  • SEND
  • MADS

Today’s Strands Answer For Game #788 On April 30, 2026

If you’ve tried everything, including using the above hints, and are still stuck, you can find the answers to today’s Strands below. Check out the answers for past puzzles here.

  • MIST
  • STEAM
  • VAPOR
  • AEROSOL
  • HUMIDITY
  • DRIZZLE
  • SPANAGRAM: CONDENSATION

If you solved today’s Strands without our answers, congratulations! If not, take note of the best tips above for tomorrow’s puzzle, and check back daily for more hints.

When Is The Next Strands Game After #788?

All New York Times games are updated daily at 12 am or Midnight in your respective time zone. Information about the next game is often available in the hours leading up to it, giving players a heads-up. The game on May 1 will be Strands Game #789.

What Is Strands?

Strands is a daily word search puzzle game created by The New York Times. Launched in 2024, Strands challenges players to discover a common theme among words in a 6×8 letter grid. The game’s original concept was developed by Juliette Seive, a research director on The New York Times Games team, with the aim of replicating Wordle’s success. Tracy Bennett serves as the puzzle editor for Strands, overseeing the creation and curation of daily puzzles since its launch.

For even more Insider Gaming delivered straight to your inbox, sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP
